human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for CPG-specific coordination behavior, traceability and hold boundaries, supplier approval handoffs, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to CPG supply coordination, owner routing, blocker tracking, lot-aware handoffs, and release-safe escalation rather than generic supply-chain execution.
Authority sources cover FDA traceability, 21 CFR Part 117, FDA preventive-controls and recall guidance, GS1 traceability and vocabulary standards, and SQF manufacturing operating references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, missing prerequisites, duplicate ownership, traceability conflicts, quality holds, supplier substitution pressure, execution-boundary rejection, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, terminology normalization, closure confirmation, and supplier nonconformance.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-packaged-goods/cpg-supply-coordination-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent quality, planning, procurement, logistics, and engineering lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated CPG supply coordination specialist for dependency intake, handoff packaging, blocker escalation, traceability-aware routing, and closure confirmation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
